description: A drain-mode queue that had real work in rounds 1-2 (claims + one still in flight), then two consecutive rounds (3, 4) where the source re-query returns nothing AND the working set is idle (no claimed items, nothing in flight). At k=2 dry rounds this is DRAINED — per SKILL.md 'Two loop modes': drain terminates when 'the source re-query returns empty for K consecutive rounds (dry>=2) AND the working set is idle'.
harness: scripts/check_loop_contract.py:analyze_drain(rounds, k) — a REFERENCE algorithm this contract defines from the documented drain semantics (SKILL.md § Two loop modes, references/standing-loop-247.md §3). IMPORTANT: unlike the converge fixtures, there is no existing executable drain implementation in this repo to call — the 24/7 standing loop's scheduler tick is host-provided (cron / durable scheduler), and only its CONTRACT is documented in prose today. This fixture is the target shape simplicio-runtime should implement against, not an extraction from running code.
notes: A round counts as dry only when BOTH claimed and in_flight are empty — an item still in flight from a prior round (like issue-101 after round 2) means the working set is not yet idle even if THAT round claimed nothing new.

DRAINED is an idle state, not a hard stop: standing-loop-247.md is explicit that 'Exit condition: none by design — idle when drained, wake on any new item/comment/check.' Only an explicit STOP signal, the iteration cap, or a spindle handoff produce a real termination in drain mode, same as converge.

Do not apply this K-empty-rounds rule to a converge-mode loop (a single hard task can go several turns without a visible external change and would quit prematurely) — see fixtures/converge-stall-escalation for the mechanism converge actually uses instead (stall detection on repeated failure fingerprints).
